recommendation.title: Easy run as planned — watch HR, not pace
recommendation.reasoning: TSB is -24.3, just above the -25 swap threshold, after a weekend spent moving apartments (loading/unloading a truck, carrying furniture up and down stairs on both Friday and Sunday) plus Saturday's long run overrunning its prescription by roughly 55%. HRV is actually 15% above the 65ms baseline and sleep score is 88 (8.1h) — both strong recovery signals — and the device's own readiness score (59) sits in the same moderate range as this assessment. Tomorrow's scheduled easy run is the right call as-is, but three of the last four tracked running sessions ran at tempo-effort HR (147-154bpm) despite hitting target pace, so effort discipline matters more than the pace window right now.
recommendation.sessionDetail: 35 min at aerobic pace (5:00–5:15/km). If HR pushes past ~142bpm at that pace, slow down further — the priority is genuine easy effort, not the stated pace range.
recommendation.bikeNote: U-Bahn both ways — TSB is deep negative enough (-24.3) to protect the legs even on an easy day, given the physical toll of the apartment move (Fri/Sun) plus Saturday's long run overrunning its prescription.

sessions.lesson: This wasn't a recovery run — it was apartment-move labor (loading/carrying furniture, stairs), which is why the device logged it under an unmapped activity type with 0km GPS distance; step-count history put the real distance at 11.38km. avg HR of 116bpm reflects sustained physical exertion from the move, not deliberate easy pacing.
Pace (5:63/km) landed close to the prescribed range, but duration and distance ran roughly 55% past the plan's 45min/~8km, and avg HR 154 was tempo effort, not the easy long-run intent.
Correctly ran slower than the week's other easy efforts given how deep TSB was running, but avg HR still hit 147bpm — above the easy zone — showing the week's fatigue hadn't fully cleared yet.
Pace 5:22/km matched the prescribed range, but avg HR 153bpm was well above the easy zone — a tempo-effort day mislabeled as easy, most likely from accumulated fatigue.

recentActivity.analysis: TSS climbed through the week and spiked hard on Saturday's long run (76.8, nearly double any other day), consistent with a base-phase week building volume — but three of the four tracked running sessions (Jul 7, 9, 11) show HR well above the prescribed easy zone despite on-target pacing, a recurring sign that accumulated fatigue (TSB -25 to -32 most of the week) is pushing 'easy' efforts into tempo territory. Worth noting Jul 12's tracked activity was apartment-move labor, not a run — the weekend move likely compounds the fatigue picture beyond what running TSS alone shows.
